title: GitHub Review by sourcery-ai[bot]
summary: The reviewer suggests two changes: (1) scope `gofumpt` to only git-tracked `.go` files (e.g. `git ls-files '*.go' | xargs gofumpt -l`) instead of the entire repo, to avoid failures on generated/vendored code; (2) wire the `GOFUMPT_VERSION` CI env var into local tooling (e.g. a `make fmt` target) so contributors stay on the same version.
external_feedback:
Hey - I've left some high level feedback:

- The `format-check` job currently runs `gofumpt -l .` over the entire repo; consider restricting this to tracked Go source files (e.g. via `git ls-files '*.go' | xargs gofumpt -l` / `-w`) to avoid unexpected failures on generated, vendored, or tooling code that may not be intended to follow the same formatting rules.
- You’ve added `GOFUMPT_VERSION` as a top-level CI env var; to keep local development consistent with CI, it may be worth wiring this same version into any existing local tooling (e.g. a `make fmt` or developer setup script) so contributors don’t unknowingly use a different gofumpt version.

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

~~~markdown
Please address the comments from this code review:

## Overall Comments
- The `format-check` job currently runs `gofumpt -l .` over the entire repo; consider restricting this to tracked Go source files (e.g. via `git ls-files '*.go' | xargs gofumpt -l` / `-w`) to avoid unexpected failures on generated, vendored, or tooling code that may not be intended to follow the same formatting rules.
- You’ve added `GOFUMPT_VERSION` as a top-level CI env var; to keep local development consistent with CI, it may be worth wiring this same version into any existing local tooling (e.g. a `make fmt` or developer setup script) so contributors don’t unknowingly use a different gofumpt version.
~~~

</details>

***

resolution_summary: Both review comments were fully addressed. The CI format-check job was changed from `gofumpt -l .` (entire repo) to `git ls-files '*.go' | xargs -r gofumpt -l` (tracked Go files only) to avoid failures on generated/vendored code. The pinned `GOFUMPT_VERSION` was wired into local development via new Makefile targets (`make fmt`, `make format-check`, `make install-gofumpt`) with a matching `GOFUMPT_VERSION ?= v0.9.2` variable. Additionally, `docs/reference/ci-workflow.md` was updated to document both changes: the tracked-files-only approach and the Makefile version parity with CI.
